title: triaenophora rupestris
intro: Triaenophora rupestris, also known as the rock triaenophora, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It is a parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by connecting to the roots of other plants, typically grasses and sedges, through its haustoria. Triaenophora rupestris has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of coughs, colds, and fever. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support respiratory health and immune function.
